topic: Ismail's school report — straight A's, so proud

turns.content: yo my son just brought home his report card bro. straight A's wallahi i almost cried
That's amazing! You must be so proud. Straight A's is no joke — he's clearly putting in the work. What grade is he in now?
ismail is in 3rd grade bro. his teacher wrote a note saying hes one of the best readers in class too. like bro this kid is smarter than me already lol
That's incredible — strong reading skills at that age set the foundation for everything else. Sounds like Ismail is really thriving. Did you do anything to celebrate with him?
took him to get ice cream after school and told him if he keeps it up ill get him the new pokemon game he been asking for
Ha, nothing motivates like a new game! That's a great reward to keep him going. Does he like school in general, or is he more of a "I do it because I have to" kid?

nah he actually likes it wallahi. especially science and math. he was showing me this project he did about the solar system and he knew all the planets and their sizes. i was like bro i didnt know half this stuff at your age

A kid who genuinely enjoys learning — that's a gift. The solar system project sounds awesome. Science and math at a young age can really open doors later on. You're clearly doing something right as a dad if he's this engaged.
he was also telling me about this book his teacher gave him. like a chapter book about animals. he read the whole thing in two days bro. i cant even get through a chapter without falling asleep lol

That's awesome — a kid who reads for fun and not just because it's assigned is something special. That habit alone is going to serve him well for the rest of his life. He's building discipline without even realizing it.
bro that means a lot. i just want him to have every opportunity u know. everything i do is for that kid. like when im tired driving at 2am i just think about his future

That's what keeps you going, and he's going to understand that one day. Kids pick up on that dedication even when they're young. The fact that he's thriving in school tells you he feels supported and secure. You should be really proud of yourself too.
wallahi i hope so. sometimes i worry im not around enough but then i see his grades and how happy he is and i think ok maybe im doing alright

The results speak for themselves. A kid who's happy, doing well in school, and excited to show his dad his report card — that's not an accident. That's a kid who knows his dad loves him. Quality matters just as much as quantity.
inshallah bro. alhamdulillah for real. ok let me go call him and say goodnight. thanks for letting me brag lol
Brag away, you've earned it! Give Ismail a high five from me. Goodnight to both of you.
